element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Slower ranged option with unlimited use
Area damage with self-harm risk; reveals hidden doors
Gating mechanic; required to access certain areas
Required for underwater areas; restricts weapons to harpoon gun
Underwater-only weapon when diving suit equipped
Obtained through obscure interaction (hit specific NPC 5 times)
Communication tool with NPCs; hints and advice
Tracks hidden Goonies; resets upon rescue
Recurring enemies; stunnable but not defeatable except Pipsqueak

typical_relief_triggers: Finding a crucial item progression tool
Exiting to first-person maze (less combat pressure)
Obtaining shoes that unlock new areas
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Exploration-driven with punctuated combat encounters; players oscillate between platform action (tension) and first-person puzzle exploration (relief)

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Strategic absence of visual elements; players infer progression from what is noticeably missing
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden doors revealed by bombs or glasses; obscure NPC interactions (e.g., hitting old woman 5 times for candle)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate; basic yo-yo and slingshot combat with spring shoes as early progression gate
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating; introduction of diving suit mechanics, Fratelli pursuits, multi-layered exploration requirements
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex; requires synthesizing implicit clues and obscure item discoveries; final Goonie locations and Annie rescue demand strategy guide-level knowledge
notable_spikes: Transition to underwater areas (diving suit requirement + weapon restriction)
Obtaining the candle (requires discovering 5-hit NPC interaction)
Progressing without Magic Locator Device refreshes

notable_level_patterns.description: Two large interconnected maps with platform and first-person duality; progression gated by item discovery rather than linear progression
Missing visual elements and strategic absences communicate where to explore next; no explicit waypoints
Each of 6 Goonies rescued increases max health; serves as clear progression marker and encourages exploration
Platform screens (action-focused) connected by first-person maze rooms (puzzle/exploration-focused); door transitions shift gameplay mode
Dual-weapon system (primary + secondary) with area-specific restrictions (e.g., underwater limits to harpoon gun)
